g.data-narration: Welcome to Process Concepts. Processes are the fundamental units of work in any operating system.
A process is a program in execution - an active entity with program counter, registers, stack, and heap memory.
g.text: A process is a program in execution. It includes:

g.data-narration: A program is passive - stored on disk. A process is active - loaded in memory with its own execution state.
Process memory layout shows how a program's memory is organized from high to low address.
g.text: Memory address space organization of a running process:

g.data-narration: The stack stores local variables, function parameters, and return addresses. It grows downward in memory.
The data segment stores initialized global variables, while the BSS segment holds uninitialized globals set to zero.
The Process Control Block (PCB) is a data structure that stores all information about a process in the OS.
g.text: The PCB is the kernel data structure that stores all information about a process:

g.data-narration: PCB also stores I/O status information, open files, device information, and scheduling details like priority.
The fork() system call creates a new process by duplicating the parent process. The child gets a copy of the address space.
g.text: Creates a new process by duplicating the parent process:

g.data-narration: The exec() system call replaces the current process memory with a new program. Often used after fork().
Process termination can be voluntary (exit) or involuntary (killed). Child processes may become orphans or zombies.

g.text: Parent died first, adopted by init
Child terminated, parent hasn't called wait()

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: A process is an active program in execution with memory layout including stack, heap, data, and text segments.
Exam tip: Know all PCB fields, memory layout from high to low address, and fork() behavior returning different values to parent and child.
g.text: • Memorize all PCB fields and their purposes
• Understand fork() returns 0 to child, child's PID to parent
🎯 Practice problems available in the app!
